title: Chapter 5 Master Quiz
description: Test your mastery over Data Structures: Arrays and ArrayLists.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of using an Array?
options: It stores code to be executed later.
It groups multiple pieces of related data into a single variable.
questions.explanation: Arrays allow you to store many items under a single variable name, reducing clutter.

questions.question: Which set of brackets do you add to a data type to declare an Array?
questions.explanation: Square brackets `[]` (e.g., `int[]`) denote an Array.

questions.question: A standard Array in Java can dynamically stretch to add more elements later in the code.
questions.explanation: Standard Arrays are fixed in size. You cannot change their length once created.

questions.question: At what number do Array indexes start counting in Java?
options: It depends on the Array size.
questions.explanation: Java is zero-indexed. The first item is always at index `0`.

questions.question: If `String[] colors = {"Red", "Blue", "Green"};`, what does `colors[2]` evaluate to?
questions.explanation: Red is 0, Blue is 1, Green is 2.

questions.question: If an array has 50 elements, what is the index of the VERY LAST element?
questions.explanation: Since indexes start at 0, the last index is always `length - 1`. So, 50 - 1 = 49.

questions.question: What well-known error happens if you try to use `colors[50]` on a 50-item array?
questions.explanation: Index 50 doesn't exist (it stops at 49). Trying to access it throws an `IndexOutOfBoundsException`.

questions.question: How do you replace the first item in an array `arr` with the number 10?
questions.explanation: You access the index specifically (`arr[0]`) and assign it a new value (`= 10`).

questions.question: What does `new int[8];` create?
options: An array holding the exact integer '8'.
An array with 8 completely empty/default slots.
questions.correctAnswer: An array with 8 completely empty/default slots.
questions.explanation: The `new` keyword followed by `[size]` creates an empty array of that specific size.

questions.question: How do you find out exactly how many items are in a standard Array named `data`?
questions.explanation: Standard Java arrays have a `.length` property (no parentheses).

questions.question: An Array's `.length` will change if you empty one of its slots.
questions.explanation: The length of the array NEVER changes, even if you erase the data inside a slot.

questions.question: What does this code do: `for(int i=0; i < arr.length; i++) { print(arr[i]); }`
options: Prints the size of the array.
Prints every item in the array, from first to last.
questions.correctAnswer: Prints every item in the array, from first to last.
questions.explanation: The index `i` increments, allowing you to peek into every single slot sequentially.

questions.question: To avoid going out of bounds in a loop, you should loop while `i < arr.____`
questions.explanation: You use `<` (strictly less than) the array's `length` property.

questions.question: What is the shortcut loop specifically designed to rip through every item in an Array?
questions.explanation: The For-Each loop (`for (Type item : array)`) simplifies array iteration safely.

questions.question: Does this For-Each syntax work? `for (String item : backpack) { print(item); }`
questions.explanation: It reads 'For each String item in the backpack array'.

questions.question: What solves the problem of standard Arrays being fixed in size?
questions.explanation: An ArrayList can dynamically stretch to add more elements and shrink to remove them.

questions.question: Which keyword do you use to place a new item at the END of an `ArrayList`?
questions.explanation: `cart.add("Apple")` pushes Apple to the end of the ArrayList.

questions.question: Since an `ArrayList` is an Object, `list[0]` is invalid syntax. How do you grab the first item instead?
questions.explanation: You use the `.get()` method provided by the ArrayList.

questions.question: Are ArrayLists required to be imported into your Java file before use?
questions.explanation: It is a utility class built into Java that must be explicitly imported.

questions.question: How do you check how many items currently exist in an `ArrayList`?
questions.explanation: Whereas standard Arrays use `.length`, the ArrayList Object uses the `.size()` method.

questions.question: To throw away an item from an ArrayList, you use the `list._____("Item")` method.
questions.explanation: The `.remove()` method removes the first occurrence of the item.
